title: Outcomes-tracking process (partner self-report) + content re-review cadence + staleness fail-safe
context: wash-guides collects no telemetry, so outcomes (reach, adoption) are tracked via partner self-report using a standard template. Content freshness is enforced by lastReviewed/validUntil fields with a staleness fail-safe. Without a documented re-review cadence and outcomes process, content will go stale silently and reach will be untracked. This task depends on a secured partner (partner-023) and the production deployment (deploy-024) to define meaningful cadence targets.
objective: Define, document, and commit: (1) a privacy-preserving partner self-report template for outcomes tracking (distinct people/households reached, channels, regions, languages, de-duplication notes); (2) a content re-review cadence (shorter for HIGH-risk units); and (3) the stale-content flagging and fail-safe process using lastReviewed/validUntil (how flagging works, who triggers re-review, what happens when a HIGH-risk unit passes validUntil).
acceptanceCriteria: Partner self-report template is committed and covers: partner name, reporting period, distribution channels, distinct people/households reached (confirmed vs. estimated, flagged as such), regions/languages, de-duplication notes across touchpoints and across partners at roll-up; template explicitly counts distinct recipients (not downloads), once per period

Content re-review cadence is documented: at minimum annual review for all units, shorter cadence for HIGH-risk dosing/clinical units; cadence is recorded in governance docs and referenced in unit validUntil dates

Stale-content fail-safe process is documented: how auto-flagging is triggered (lastReviewed + validUntil); what happens when a HIGH-risk unit passes validUntil (withheld from the published site until re-verified and re-signed-off); who is responsible for initiating re-review
Outcomes process is privacy-preserving: no in-app telemetry, no PII; all reach data is partner-attested self-report using the template
Document reviewed and approved by a maintainer and the project steward (once secured) distinct from the author
output: Committed governance documents: a partner self-report outcomes template, a content re-review cadence document, and a stale-content fail-safe process document.
